metadata.title: Jacketed Heating and Cooling Tanks | Welltech®
metadata.description: Stainless steel jacketed and insulated tanks designed to precisely control product temperature in chemical, food, and pharmaceutical processes.

hero.title: Jacketed (Heating/Cooling) Tanks
hero.desc: Reactors and tanks with half-pipe coil, roll-bond, and double-wall technologies featuring high heat transfer efficiency that provide the precise temperature control your process needs.

technology.title: Heat Transfer and Jacket Technologies
technology.desc: Jacketed tanks are systems that have a secondary cavity (jacket) integrated into their body to heat or cool the fluid inside. Welltech® Engineering calculates the optimal surface area according to your process's heating/cooling rate and the fluid to be used (steam, thermal oil, glycol, chilled water). While Half-pipe coil is ideal for high-pressure steam, Roll-bond (Dimple) jackets provide excellent efficiency in homogeneous cooling and heating applications.

features.title: Design and Insulation Advantages
items: Special Jacket Designs Providing Homogeneous Heat Distribution
Welds Tested for High-Pressure Steam and Thermal Oil
100% Energy Efficient Insulation with Polyurethane or Rock Wool
Fully Welded Stainless Outer Cladding Over Insulation
Thermal Break Connections to Prevent Heat Loss
Compatibility with Precise PT-100 Thermocouple and Heat Control Automation
features.imgAlt: Jacketed Tank Insulation and Heat Transfer

table.title: Jacket Types and Technical Applications
table.note: * Operating pressure and jacket sheet thicknesses are statically calculated according to (ASME Section VIII or EN 13445) standards.

items.q: What is the difference between a Half-Pipe Coil jacket and a Double Wall?
items.a: A half-pipe jacket consists of pipes wrapped and welded around the shell. Its high-pressure (e.g., 10 bar steam) resistance is excellent. A double wall is a second sheet that completely surrounds the tank, more suitable for low-pressure (atmospheric) water circulation.

items.q: How is the insulation material chosen?
items.a: While Polyurethane foam is used to prevent condensation in cooling (chiller) processes, non-combustible Rock Wool or Ceramic Wool insulation is preferred in high-temperature (steam, thermal oil) heating processes.

cta.title: Which Jacket Type?
cta.desc: Let us know your targeted reaction time and heating/cooling fluid, and let's choose the safest jacket system together.
